description: Resamples the audio signal and processes its child nodes with a different sample rate

parameterGroups.label: Resampling Configuration
parameterGroups.rationale: Both parameters control the resampling behavior: the pitch ratio and the interpolation quality.

properties.note: UI-only layout hint. Does not affect processing.

gaps.question: Confirm the wrap::repitch processing flow: (1) compute internal sample count = round(numSamples / ratio), (2) downsample input into internal buffer per channel, (3) child processes internal buffer, (4) upsample result back to original buffer. Is the internal buffer allocated during prepare() (blockSize * MaxDownsampleFactor = blockSize * 2)?
gaps.context: The resampling flow is the core feature. Users need to understand the relationship between RepitchFactor and effective sample rate.

gaps.question: Confirm the three interpolation types: 0=Cubic (CatmullRom, highest quality), 1=Linear (mid quality), 2=None (ZeroOrderHold, lowest quality). The base data has an encoded TextToValueConverter for the Interpolation parameter -- confirm these labels match.
gaps.context: Users need clear guidance on quality vs CPU tradeoff for each interpolation type.

gaps.question: The infrastructure doc says repitch only works with 1 or 2 channels (hardcoded switch in process()). Confirm this limitation. What happens if placed in a multi-channel context (>2 channels)?
gaps.context: Critical constraint that must be documented.

gaps.question: Confirm that repitch asserts false in processFrame(). This means repitch containers cannot be nested inside frame-based containers.
gaps.context: Important constraint for users building nested container hierarchies.

gaps.question: The phase3 doc says factor > 1.0 upsamples internally so child sees lower frequency, and factor < 1.0 does the opposite. Confirm this inverse relationship: higher factor = more internal samples = children perceive lower pitch.
gaps.context: The non-intuitive direction of the factor needs clear documentation.

notes: Repitch wraps DynamicSerialProcessor in wrap::repitch<T, wrap::interpolators::dynamic>. HasFixedParameters=true in cppProperties. The skew of 0.63 on RepitchFactor creates a logarithmic feel centered around 1.0 (no change). The range is limited to one octave (0.5-2.0) but the phase3 doc notes that stacking repitch containers can extend the range. The Interpolation parameter has an encoded TextToValueConverter for the three mode labels. Uses SimpleReadWriteLock for thread-safe parameter changes.
